elements.title: The Luck of Cumberland
elements.contents: ... that The Luck of Cumberland (pictured), designed by Herbert Maryon and executed by the Keswick School of Industrial Art, is made of more than 200 ounces of solid silver?

elements.title: Roy Waldo Miner
elements.contents: ... that Roy Waldo Miner extracted about forty tons of coral from the Andros Barrier Reef to build a museum diorama?

elements.title: Waahine Toa
elements.contents: ... that the Waahine Toa paintings were criticised as unfashionable when first exhibited in the 1980s, but are now considered iconic in New Zealand art?

elements.title: Valentin Senn
elements.contents: ... that NFL player Valentin Senn used to play the trumpet in the Swarovski Orchestra?

elements.title: an autobiographical novel
elements.contents: ... that B. S. Johnson based an autobiographical novel on his three-week voyage on a fishing trawler?

elements.title: Liz Stooke
elements.contents: ... that Liz Stooke worked as a receptionist at the London Drama School before becoming an actress?

elements.title: WCBQ
elements.contents: ... that the North Carolina radio stations WCBQ and WHNC were fined by a federal judge for broadcasting songs without a license?

elements.title: Horizon Zero Dawn: The Frozen Wilds
elements.contents: ... that animators for Horizon Zero Dawn: The Frozen Wilds studied polar bears and red pandas for machine movements, and mixed synthetic noises with walrus and seal grunts for their vocalisations?

elements.title: Potters Bar Tunnel
elements.contents: ... that Thomas Brassey paid his workers with illegal tokens while building the Potters Bar Tunnel?
